mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already near zero, the discount-rate channel dominates and lifts equity valuations over the next month. A lower policy rate should precede positive S&P 500 forward returns, and the effect should weaken once rates stop declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if triggered easing periods do not beat the unconditional baseline by at least 2 percentage points in cumulative return.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and tighter financial conditions, which should depress near-term risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when unemployment is still rising from an established trough.
failure_condition: Reject if, after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases or average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS identifies broadening labor income and retail demand, which should support earnings expectations and push SPX higher over a one-quarter horizon. The effect is not attributable to either series alone but to their joint confirmation of expanding final demand.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform single-series signals, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial supply capacity and stronger cyclical output, which should translate into positive SPX returns over the next week through better earnings momentum and risk sentiment. The signal fails if production gains are offset by demand weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX returns are not positive following month-over-month increases in INDPRO, or if the sign of the effect reverses in more than half of rolling 1-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST marks housing weakness and an impending slowdown in consumer demand and credit creation, which should pressure equities lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when housing starts break below their recent trend rather than when they drift sideways.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are not negative after HOUST drops at least 10% below its 3-month average, or if the average return after the trigger is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains extremely low, real policy rates become more negative and financial conditions ease in real terms, supporting equities over the next month. The signal should work only when inflation is accelerating faster than policy rates can respond.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-over-policy condition does not correspond to positive 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are negative in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series during a period of elevated UNRATE indicates that employment is recovering faster than the headline unemployment rate suggests, which should be bullish for SPX over a 60-day horizon. The mechanism fails if payroll growth is too weak to offset labor slack.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.5% and PAYEMS rising for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-recovery condition does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the average return is below the unconditional 60-day mean by more than 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals synchronized broad demand growth across production and consumption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following quarter through stronger revenue expectations. The effect should disappear when GDP growth and retail sales move in opposite directions.
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal fails to outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests.
